quotes.verbatim_quote:
When any one of the voices in a triad is raised one step of the
scale it is placed in super position.

When it is lowered one step of the scale it is placed in sub
position.
quotes.why_load_bearing: The core definitional move of the chapter—establishes 'super' and 'sub' as the diatonic-step displacements that generate every voicing in the series.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
When the voice motion in a triad (regardless of direction,)
moves one step above normal, or, returns from above normal
to normal position, it is termed “super” motion—

When the voice motion moves one step below normal, or,
returns from below normal to normal position, it is termed
“sub” motion.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Reframes super/sub as motion rather than static position, the conceptual key to reading the chromatic inner-voice exercises as melodic events.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
A first inversion triad with the middle voice super automat-
ically becomes a member of the second inversion family—
A first inversion with the upper voice sub becomes a member
of the root position triads—

Asecond inversion triad with the lower voice super becomes
a root position triad—

A root position triad with the lower voice sub becomes a
second inversion triad, and so on—
quotes.why_load_bearing: States the mutation rule that makes the super/sub series mathematically self-contained: every voice motion lands on another named inversion in the family.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
When working with the super and sub principle it is
necessary to keep the named inversion in each study in mind
at all times because “it” is the “origin”; it is the original
voicing that is going to produce others. Therefore, it becomes
the “boss” — it is the dominating factor regardless of the other
inversions produced.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Establishes the conceptual-vs-notational distinction that lets identical notations be read differently depending on which inversion 'caused' them.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
SUPER AND SUB CONCEPT - All chords are centers - When voices in a chord are moved one
step up or down in any scale the resulting change becomes another center.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Articulates the generative axiom of Van Eps's harmonic system: every voicing is both a destination and a launchpad for new centers.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
There is a distinction without a difference which is very similar to the enharmonic difference in notes
by the use of accidentals.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Frames the conceptual identity of super/sub products with enharmonic spelling—same sound, different functional meaning—a central interpretive lens for the series.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Every wrong note plants the germ of a bad habit because the ear retains the sound of the mistake. and therefore it becomes subconsciously
influential; there is a very strong chance that the mistake will be repeated, and that is why any new study should be gone over very slowly and
correctly.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Justifies the slow-practice doctrine on ear-memory grounds, defining the discipline required by the multi-key repetition format that follows.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Repetition in fingering is necessary in all studies that are shown just descending or ascending, because they must be read in reverse to return.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Explains the bidirectional reading requirement that the chapter's one-direction notation implicitly demands.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Alternating string sets as shown above and else where in these studies is necessary practise
for building hand shift accuracy plus the ability to change sets either up or down any time or
place within range
quotes.why_load_bearing: Names the pedagogical purpose of the chapter's set-jumping notation: hand-shift accuracy and on-demand set-changing across the fingerboard.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Variations - the reason for using 3 fingers to sound triads such as bar 1 is obvious in the
Examples below
quotes.why_load_bearing: Introduces the rationale (about to be shown) for preferring three-finger triad fingering over barres in certain super/sub contexts where voice motion is coming.
